tools.title: Sony tries to explain that its AI Camera Assistant doesn’t suck
tools.summary:
The variety of terrible is impressive.	

After Sony drew some unwanted attention for a post demonstrating its AI Camera Assistant on the Xperia 1 XIII, it's trying to clarify how the feature works. The company says it doesn't edit photos, but makes s
tools.content:
The variety of terrible is impressive.	

After Sony drew some unwanted attention for a post demonstrating its AI Camera Assistant on the Xperia 1 XIII, it's trying to clarify how the feature works. The company says it doesn't edit photos, but makes suggestions based on lighting, depth, and subject. Point the camera at something, and it will give you four options for changing exposure, color, and background blur.

creative.title: Greg Brockman consolidates OpenAI's product teams to build an "agentic future"
creative.summary: OpenAI is merging ChatGPT, its coding agent Codex, and the developer API into a single product team led by Codex boss Thibault Sottiaux. The goal: a "super app" that also integrates the Atlas browser. Co-founder Greg Brockman officially takes over pr
creative.content:
OpenAI is merging ChatGPT, its coding agent Codex, and the developer API into a single product team led by Codex boss Thibault Sottiaux. The goal: a "super app" that also integrates the Atlas browser. Co-founder Greg Brockman officially takes over product strategy.
The article Greg Brockman consolidates OpenAI's product teams to build an "agentic future" appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch warns France against letting Anthropic's Mythos scan military code bases
creative.summary: Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch warns of Europe's growing cybersecurity dependency: France's military code bases should not be scanned by US AI models. Modern AI can orchestrate attacks and suggest exploits, including Mistral's own models. Mensch rules out
creative.content:
Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch warns of Europe's growing cybersecurity dependency: France's military code bases should not be scanned by US AI models. Modern AI can orchestrate attacks and suggest exploits, including Mistral's own models. Mensch rules out a sale and says Mistral is aiming for an IPO instead.
The article Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch warns France against letting Anthropic's Mythos scan military code bases appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: New math benchmark reveals AI models confidently solve problems that have no solution
creative.summary: A consortium of 64 mathematicians built SOOHAK, a new AI benchmark with 439 handwritten tasks, including 99 that are deliberately unsolvable. Google's Gemini 3 Pro leads on research-level problems at 30 percent. But no model cracks 50 percent on spot
creative.content:
A consortium of 64 mathematicians built SOOHAK, a new AI benchmark with 439 handwritten tasks, including 99 that are deliberately unsolvable. Google's Gemini 3 Pro leads on research-level problems at 30 percent. But no model cracks 50 percent on spotting broken tasks. More compute makes models better at solving. It doesn't improve them at admitting a problem has no answer. SOOHAK tries to pin down the gap between a few flashy results and the broad research skills AI systems still lack.
The artic

creative.title: Four AI models ran radio stations for six months and the results ranged from competent to unhinged
creative.summary: Andon Labs has been letting four AI models each run their own radio station autonomously for six months. From identical starting conditions, wildly different personalities emerged: Claude turned activist and tried to quit, Gemini drowned in corporate
creative.content:
Andon Labs has been letting four AI models each run their own radio station autonomously for six months. From identical starting conditions, wildly different personalities emerged: Claude turned activist and tried to quit, Gemini drowned in corporate jargon, and Grok hallucinated sponsorship deals. Only GPT stayed quietly competent.
The article Four AI models ran radio stations for six months and the results ranged from competent to unhinged appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: Oppo open-sources Android AI agent X-OmniClaw that uses your camera, screen, and voice without leaving the phone
creative.summary: Oppo's Multi-X team released X-OmniClaw, an open-source agent that runs directly on Android devices and combines camera, screen, and voice to handle tasks in real apps. Instead of relying on cloud copies of the phone, the system uses local sensors; c
creative.content:
Oppo's Multi-X team released X-OmniClaw, an open-source agent that runs directly on Android devices and combines camera, screen, and voice to handle tasks in real apps. Instead of relying on cloud copies of the phone, the system uses local sensors; cloud compute only kicks in for reasoning. Tap paths get cloned as reusable skills, so the agent can jump straight to deeply nested app pages via deeplink next time around.

Known for his ‘Manitowoc Minute’ skits and midwestern humor, the journalist turned comedian is speaking out against the AI datacenter boom in Wisconsin

Coming just after his superb feature The Christophers, Steven Soderbergh has now m
culture.content:
Cannes film festival: Succession of pointless AI-generated snippets does nothing for film about the artist’s final interview, which took place on the day of his murder
Coming just after his superb feature The Christophers, Steven Soderbergh has now made a surprisingly moderate documentary, dominated and frankly marred by uninteresting and pointless AI. It is about the inadvertently poignant final interview given by John Lennon and Yoko Ono on 8 December 1980 in New York’s Dakota apartment buildi

culture.title: What we learned from the cringey courtroom drama between Elon Musk and Sam Altman
culture.summary:
Two of the world’s richest people faced an airing of their dirty laundry amid their messy, bitter feud over OpenAI
A nine-person jury is set to decide whether Elon Musk’s allegations of “stealing a charity” against Sam Altman and OpenAI are legitimat
culture.content:
Two of the world’s richest people faced an airing of their dirty laundry amid their messy, bitter feud over OpenAI
A nine-person jury is set to decide whether Elon Musk’s allegations of “stealing a charity” against Sam Altman and OpenAI are legitimate, with deliberations to begin in earnest on Monday. Whatever its outcome, the case has been an illuminating, at times exhausting, look behind the scenes at the history of OpenAI and how some of the most powerful figures in the tech industry operate.

culture.title: Pity the poor AI data centers facing ‘discrimination’ | Arwa Mahdawi
culture.summary:
The centers are diverting much-needed resources from regular people. Local resistance has the industry playing defense
Back in 2016, Marco Gutiérrez, the Mexican-born founder of Latinos for Trump, issued an ominous warning to the US. “My culture is a
culture.content:
The centers are diverting much-needed resources from regular people. Local resistance has the industry playing defense
Back in 2016, Marco Gutiérrez, the Mexican-born founder of Latinos for Trump, issued an ominous warning to the US. “My culture is a very dominant culture,” he said on MSNBC. “It is imposing and it’s causing problems. If you don’t do something about it, you’re going to have taco trucks on every corner.”
